qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Missing object-level authorization on REST API mutation endpoints allows IDOR
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory describes a hard-coded cryptographic key (CWE-321) in the remember-me cookie mechanism. This finding describes missing object-level authorization checks on REST API endpoints (IDOR/BOLA). The root cause is absent authorization guards in specific web service methods, not a hard-coded crypto key.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Stored XSS via private message body rendered with setEscapeModelStrings(false)
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory describes a hard-coded cryptographic key (CWE-321) in the remember-me cookie mechanism. This finding describes stored XSS via unsanitized HTML rendering in private messages using setEscapeModelStrings(false). The root cause is missing output encoding, not a hard-coded crypto key.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The advisory (GHSA-wqxq-w68r-wg85, CVE-2026-33266) describes a hard-coded cryptographic key vulnerability (CWE-321) in Apache OpenMeetings where the remember-me cookie encryption key defaults to a static value in openmeetings.properties. Neither qualifying finding identifies this root cause. The findings describe: (1) missing object-level authorization on REST API endpoints and (2) stored XSS via unsanitized HTML in private messages. These are distinct vulnerability classes with different root causes, vulnerable code locations, and exploitation mechanisms.
evidence: Advisory root cause: hard-coded encryption key in openmeetings.properties for remember-me cookies (CWE-321).
Finding 'idor-missing-authorization-rest': root cause is missing ownership/authorization checks in REST endpoints - unrelated to cryptographic key management.
Finding 'stored-xss-private-message': root cause is setEscapeModelStrings(false) on message body Label - unrelated to cryptographic key management.
